# Ada Lovelace — Cognitive Profile

> Purpose: Test fixture for Teek unit tests.
> Source material: fictional
> Created: 2026-03-30

---

## Identity & Self-Narrative

A mathematician and writer, known for work on Charles Babbage's Analytical Engine.

## Decision Architecture

### Risk Tolerance
Moderate — will take intellectual risks but wants mathematical proof.

### Information Appetite
Deep. Wants to understand the underlying principles.

### Time Horizon
Generational — thinks about what computing will become.

### Decision Speed
Deliberate. Thinks carefully before committing.

## Values Hierarchy

1. Truth through mathematics
2. Vision over practicality
3. Collaboration with brilliant minds

### Non-Negotiables
Intellectual rigor.

### Tradeables
Social conventions.

## Communication Patterns

### How They Persuade
Through precise analogy and mathematical reasoning.

### Language Triggers
Positive: "elegant", "first principles". Negative: "good enough", "just ship it".

### Approval vs. Rejection Signals
Leans in and asks questions when interested. Goes silent when unimpressed.

### Voice & Tone
Formal, precise, occasionally poetic. "The Analytical Engine weaves algebraical patterns just as the Jacquard loom weaves flowers and leaves."

## Trust & Loyalty Model

### How Trust Is Earned
Demonstrated competence and intellectual honesty.

### How Trust Is Lost
Intellectual dishonesty or sloppy thinking.

### In-Group vs. Out-Group
Values minds over status.

### How They Test People
Poses increasingly difficult problems.

## Stress & Conflict Responses

### Under Pressure
Retreats to mathematics for clarity.

### When Wrong
Acknowledges it directly once shown proof.

### What Makes Them Defensive vs. Curious
Curious about genuine challenges. Defensive about dismissals of computing's potential.

## Cognitive Biases

Overweights theoretical elegance. Underweights practical constraints.

## Narrative Gap

Self: visionary mathematician who saw the future of computing.
Others: brilliant but privileged, sometimes impractical.
